description.task: Write a Java program that finds the minimum, maximum, and average of a given integer array.
instructions: Use a single for loop to compute sum, min, and max simultaneously.
Use scores.length to get the array size — do not hardcode 8.
Cast to double before dividing to get a decimal average.
Print all three lines in the exact format shown.
description.hint: Inside the loop: if (scores[i] < min) min = scores[i]; similarly for max. Add scores[i] to sum on every iteration.

description.task: Write a Java program that declares a 3x3 integer matrix and prints it in a grid format using nested for loops.
instructions: Use nested for loops: outer loop for rows, inner loop for columns.
After each row, use System.out.println() to move to the next line.
Print all 9 values in 3 rows of 3.
description.hint: Outer loop: for (int i = 0; i < matrix.length; i++). Inner loop: for (int j = 0; j < matrix[i].length; j++).

description.task: Write a Java program that sorts an integer array using Arrays.sort() and then uses Arrays.binarySearch() to find a target value.
description.context: java.util.Arrays provides utility methods for array operations. binarySearch() only works correctly on sorted arrays.
instructions: Call Arrays.sort(numbers) to sort the array in-place.
Call Arrays.binarySearch(numbers, 47) and store the result in 'index'.
Print all three lines in the exact format shown.
Do NOT change the initial array values.
description.hint: After sorting, numbers becomes [3, 12, 28, 47, 55, 64, 99]. 47 is at index 3.
